subject: [PATCH RFC 3/3] locking: Wire up contended_release tracepoint

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that trace_contended_release() is called after the lock has been released, which may lead to a false positive in the contended release tracepoint

Reviewer Steven Rostedt noted that using if statements for tracepoints without a static branch can lead to inefficient code paths, suggesting the use of trace_contended_release_enabled() and rt_mutex_has_waiters(rtm) to conditionally enable the tracepoint

reviewer expressed agreement with the patch, using a single-word affirmative
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed,  4 Mar 2026 16:56:17 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

---

Hmm, if statements should never be used just for tracepoints without a
static branch. The above should be:

	if (trace_contended_release_enabled() && rt_mutex_has_waiters(rtm))
		trace_contended_release(rwb);

The above "trace_contened_release_enabled()" is a static_branch where it
turns the if statement into a nop when the tracepoint is not enabled, and a
jmp when it is.

---

Same here.

-- Steve

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: Reviewer Peter Zijlstra pointed out that the trace_contended_release() calls will be executed twice due to static branches not being optimized by compilers, and suggested using a __pure function instead.

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the trace_contended_release() call in percpu_up_write() always fires, even when there are no blocked waiters being woken, and requested a fix to only fire it when necessary

reviewer suggested implementing the tracepoint for contended release, but noted that it shouldn't be too hard and offered to look at it or have someone else try
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, 5 Mar 2026 18:42:23 +0100
Peter Zijlstra <peterz@infradead.org> wrote:

---

Makes sense, and that could probably be done. It shouldn't be too hard to
do. If I find some time I could look at it, or perhaps someone lurking on
this thread could possibly give it a try! (I may even Cc some people that
want to learn this code).

-- Steve

2026-03-05.patch_summary: This patch adds calls to the `trace_contended_release` function in various slowpath unlock paths of sleepable locks, including mutexes, rtmutexes, semaphores, and rwsems. The purpose is to fire a tracepoint when there are blocked waiters being woken during an unlock operation. This allows for better tracing and debugging of lock contention issues.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch adds calls to the `trace_contended_release` function in various slowpath unlock paths of sleepable locks, including mutexes, rtmutexes, semaphores, and rwsems. The purpose is to fire a tracepoint when there are blocked waiters being woken during an unlock operation. This allows for better tracing and debugging of lock contention issues.
